cases.query: Write one paragraph on human-AI interaction in visual culture.
cases.answer: Human–AI interaction in visual culture blurs the boundary between maker and machine, shifting the creative process from singular authorship to co‑creation. Algorithms now curate feeds, generate imagery, and even mimic aesthetic intent, altering how audiences interpret visual meaning. This collaboration challenges traditional hierarchies—artist, tool, viewer—turning perception itself into a feedback loop between human taste and machine pattern‑recognition. In practice, every swipe, prompt, or selection shapes a shared aesthetic language, where style and agency evolve through continual exchange rather than isolated inspiration.
